frozen_components: language model (agent and world model roles)
architecture.summary: RAP (Reasoning via Planning) repurposes an LLM to serve dual roles: a reasoning agent that incrementally builds a reasoning tree, and a world model that predicts future states and rewards. A Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) algorithm orchestrates exploration of the reasoning space, using the LLM's state-prediction and reward-scoring capabilities to balance exploration versus exploitation and guide the search toward high-reward reasoning paths. The LLM generates reasoning steps, the world model scores trajectories and predicts outcomes, and MCTS selects which reasoning branches to expand next.

biblio.title: Reasoning with Language Model is Planning with World Model

claims.statement: RAP on LLaMA-33B surpasses CoT on GPT-4 with 33% relative improvement in plan generation

RAP demonstrates superiority over strong baselines including CoT and least-to-most prompting with self-consistency on a variety of challenging reasoning problems such as plan generation, math reasoning, and logical inference

LLMs lack an internal world model for predicting world states and simulating long-term action outcomes, which prevents them from performing deliberate planning akin to human brains involving exploring alternative reasoning paths, anticipating future states and rewards, and iteratively refining reasoning steps

RAP incorporates a principled planning algorithm based on Monte Carlo Tree Search for strategic exploration in the vast reasoning space, balancing exploration versus exploitation to achieve high-reward reasoning paths efficiently
